title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · Graph Lens: Gita Integrity Preservation
summary: A long-lived repository grows unexpectedly, contains unreachable objects, and reports a missing tree after interrupted maintenance; the team needs causal storage evidence before cleanup. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita integrity preservation increment in object-database integrity and reachability report. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.
Use rev-parse, cat-file, verify-pack, count-objects, and batch inspection to answer bounded questions without checkout or execution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Integrity Preservation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G44 is a theory in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Integrity Preservation contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for quarantine suspect storage, preserve alternate copies and refs, verify object connectivity, and defer destructive pruning until recovery proof exists.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G44 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation. A defensible solution applies Gita Integrity Preservation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to object-database integrity and reachability report.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 2
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case

steps.title: Build Gita Integrity Preservation evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-15uu81" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Integrity Preservation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-15uu81
# scenario-key: trace-15uu81 probe-18uu51 boundary-uu8115 owner-15uu81x fault-15uu81z
# before-ref: main=a11ce15 candidate=b00b181
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11ce15 candidate=c0ffee1
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
